company.name: Cursor
company.tagline: 24 months from a VS Code fork to a $29B valuation
company.summary: Cursor wasn't an overnight viral hit. It's a two-stage curve: 22 months of slow burn, then 13 months of vertical growth. Every valuation jump rode a narrative moment — and the narrative moments were not luck. They were the systematic byproduct of the founders treating themselves as media properties.

events.title: Anysphere founded
events.description: Four MIT undergrads (Truell / Sanger / Asif / Lunnemark) incorporate Anysphere. Initial direction: AI autocomplete for CAD software.

events.title: Pre-seed $400K
events.description: Earliest funding to begin development.

events.title: Pivot from CAD to coding
events.description: Realized CAD market was stagnant and that GitHub Copilot's plugin-on-VS-Code architecture was structurally limited. Decided to fork the entire VS Code instead of writing a plugin.

events.title: Cursor v0 launch
events.description: Public release of Cursor as a VS Code fork — an AI-native editor, not a plugin riding inside another IDE.

events.title: Seed $8M
events.description: Led by OpenAI Startup Fund. Angels include Nat Friedman (former GitHub CEO) and Arash Ferdowsi (Dropbox co-founder).

events.title: Cursor Tab (Copilot++)
events.description: First feature that's clearly better than GitHub Copilot. Becomes the core daily-driver delight.

events.title: Series A $60M / $400M
events.description: Led by a16z and Thrive Capital. TechCrunch exclusive — the first time Cursor uses an 'exclusive leak' format that becomes the template for every subsequent round.

events.title: 8-year-old codes w/ Cursor
events.description: Cloudflare VP Ricky Robinett tweets a video of his 8-year-old daughter building a chatbot in 45 minutes. 2.7M views. Boosted by Tobi Lutke, Karpathy, Lenny Rachitsky.

events.title: Lex Fridman #447
events.description: All four founders, 2h 38m. The crossover moment from dev-twitter darling to 'serious AI company' in the eyes of investors and non-dev tech audiences.

events.title: Composer v1 ships
events.description: Multi-file editing. Moves Cursor from 'better autocomplete' to 'AI that edits across your whole codebase'.

events.title: Acquires Supermaven
events.description: Buys Jacob Jackson (former Tabnine founder + ex-OpenAI) and his team. One move = tech + talent + competitor removal + 'consolidator' narrative.

events.title: Series B $105M / $2.6B
events.description: Led by Thrive Capital. Valuation jumps 6.5x in four months — almost entirely from narrative repricing, not revenue.

events.title: $100M ARR
events.description: 21 months from public launch. The fastest SaaS company in history to reach this milestone.

events.title: Karpathy 'vibe coding' tweet
events.description: 4.5M+ views. Karpathy names Cursor Composer + Sonnet as the example. Cursor becomes the de facto referent for the term — later inducted into the Collins Dictionary.

events.title: 1M DAU · 14K enterprise
events.description: Crosses one million daily active users. Fast Company World's 50 Most Innovative Companies, ranked #26.

events.title: Series C $900M / $9.9B
events.description: Led by Thrive, with Accel, a16z, DST. $500M ARR disclosed simultaneously — funding announcement + revenue milestone fired together for compound coverage.

events.title: Pro pricing controversy
events.description: Pro tier usage caps trigger community backlash. The only meaningful public-relations dent in the entire arc — fully absorbed by subsequent growth.

events.title: Cursor 2.0 + own Composer model
events.description: Multi-agent architecture, up to 8 agents in parallel, 4x speed. The shift from 'application layer' to 'application + own model' that supports the next valuation tier.

events.title: Series D $2.3B / $29.3B
events.description: Co-led by Accel and Coatue. Strategic participation from Google and Nvidia.

events.title: $1B ARR
events.description: The fastest SaaS to ever reach $1B ARR — 24 months from $1M.

events.title: $2B ARR
events.description: The doubling pace continues. ARR roughly 2x every two months across the 2025–2026 stretch.

events.title: xAI $60B option deal
events.description: xAI announces an option to acquire Cursor for $60B by year-end, or pay $10B for ongoing collaboration.
